title: node-postgres Pools, Queries, Transactions, and Failure Semantics · Type Recall
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs node-postgres pools, queries, transactions, and failure semantics from bounded code and request evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Create one bounded pg Pool per service role with connection, idle, acquisition, maximum-lifetime, error, statistics, readiness, and pool.end behavior.
Use parameterized values, allowlisted identifiers, explicit columns, row validation, cardinality checks, timeouts, and cancellation without SQL string concatenation.
Acquire and release a client exactly once with finally while distinguishing acquisition, query, protocol, server, timeout, cancellation, and application failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Pg Pool Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
Order the Pg Pool Lifecycle Node and Express server HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

content.text: A review team handles case 8455 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify pool, transaction, release, and ambiguous-commit evidence without blind replay; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one bounded pg Pool per service role with connection, idle, acquisition, maximum-lifetime, error, statistics, readiness, and pool.end behavior.
